= ISO/TC 211 Schemas

image:https://github.com/ISO-TC211/schemas/workflows/ci/badge.svg["CI status", link="https://github.com/ISO-TC211/schemas/actions?workflow=ci"]


== Introduction

This is the official repository of ISO/TC 211 XML and JSON schemas,
jointly managed by ISO/TC 211/XMG and Ribose.

The schemas are served at https://schemas.isotc211.org/ and browsable
through interactive schema browsers:

* XML schemas — generated by https://lutaml.github.io/lutaml-xsd/[LutaML XSD]
* JSON schemas — generated by https://github.com/lutaml/lutaml-jsonschema/[LutaML JSON Schema]


== CI Pipeline

Every push and pull request runs validation (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`):

. **Manifest validation** — `tools/validate_manifests.rb` checks that
  `lxr_packages.yml` and `ljr_packages.yml` have valid YAML structure,
  required keys (`name`, `title`, `standard`, `files`), no duplicate names,
  and all referenced files exist on disk.
. **XSD well-formedness** — `xmllint --noout` on every `.xsd` file.
. **JSON well-formedness** — `python3 -m json.tool` on every `.json` file.

On push to `main`, after validation passes, a `repository_dispatch` event
triggers a rebuild of the https://github.com/ISO-TC211/schemas.isotc211.org[site repo],
which generates the interactive browsers and deploys to GitHub Pages.


== Directory structure

All XSD schema files follow the `standard/-part/module/version/` pattern:

[source]
----
{standard}/{-part}/{module}/{version}/
  {module}.xsd              # Main schema entry point (3-letter name)
  *.xsd                     # Additional schema files
  *.sch                     # Schematron validation rules
  index.adoc                # Per-module documentation
  *.png                     # UML/diagram images
  examples/                 # Example XML instances
    *.xml

{standard}/resources/
  transforms/               # XSLT transform stylesheets
  codelists/                # Codelist dictionary XML files
  schematron/               # Cross-cutting Schematron rules
  bundles/                  # ZIP download packages
  namespace-tools/          # Namespace information and tools
  tools/                    # Helper scripts and utilities
  CHANGES.md                # Per-standard maintenance log

json/{standard}/{-part}/{module}/{version}/
  *.json                    # JSON Schema definitions
  examples/                 # JSON example instances
  tools/                    # Validation scripts and helpers
----

=== Naming conventions

|===
| Component | Pattern | Example

| Standard
| 5 digits
| `19115`

| Part
| `-1`, `-2`, `-3`; use `-` for standalone
| `-1`

| Module
| 3 lowercase letters matching main XSD
| `cit`, `mdb`, `gml`

| Version
| Semantic version
| `1.0`, `1.3.0`, `2.2.0`
|===


== Build dependencies

`Gemfile` defines the Ruby gems needed for building LXR/LJR packages:

* `lutaml-xsd` — parses XSD files into LXR packages
* `lutaml-jsonschema` — generates JSON schema browsers
* `lutaml-model`, `moxml`, `canon` — supporting libraries

The site repo (`schemas.isotc211.org`) references this Gemfile via
`BUNDLE_GEMFILE=schemas/Gemfile` when running lutaml commands.


== Package manifests

Packages define which schemas get grouped into a single interactive browser:

`lxr_packages.yml`::
  XSD (LXR) packages. Each package groups one or more XSD modules from the
  same standard+part+version into one browser. Provides human metadata
  (title, description, status).

`ljr_packages.yml`::
  JSON (LJR) packages. Each package groups one or more JSON schema files
  into one browser.

The manifests are validated by CI on every push. See the <<_how_to_add_a_new_xsd_schema,How to add>> sections below for required fields.


== How to add a new XSD schema

. Create the directory:
+
[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p 19115/-1/new/1.0
----

. Place the schema files:
+
[source]
----
19115/-1/new/1.0/
  new.xsd           # Main entry point (3-letter name)
  newType.xsd       # Additional type definitions
----

. Add Schematron rules (optional):
+
[source]
----
19115/-1/new/1.0/new.sch
----

. Add example instances (optional):
+
[source,sh]
----
mkdir 19115/-1/new/1.0/examples
# Place .xml files in examples/
----

. Add documentation (optional):
+
[source]
----
19115/-1/new/1.0/index.adoc
----

. Register in `lxr_packages.yml`:
+
[source,yaml]
----
- name: "19115-new-1.0"
  title: "ISO 19115-1 — New Module (new v1.0)"
  standard: "19115"
  status: "current"   # or "draft", "historical"
  description: "Description of this schema module."
  files:
    - "19115/-1/new/1.0/new.xsd"
----

. Run `ruby tools/validate_manifests.rb` to verify.

. Push — CI validates automatically. The site rebuilds and deploys.


== How to add a new JSON schema

. Create the directory:
+
[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p json/19115/-4/new/1.0
----

. Place the schema files:
+
[source]
----
json/19115/-4/new/1.0/
  new.json           # Schema definition
----

. Add examples (optional):
+
[source,sh]
----
mkdir json/19115/-4/new/1.0/examples
# Place .json files in examples/
----

. Register in `ljr_packages.yml`:
+
[source,yaml]
----
- name: "19115-4-json-1.0"
  title: "ISO 19115-4 — New Module JSON (v1.0)"
  standard: "19115"
  status: "current"
  description: "Description of this JSON schema."
  files:
    - "json/19115/-4/new/1.0/new.json"
----

. Run `ruby tools/validate_manifests.rb` to verify.


== How to add resources

=== XSLT transforms

[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p {standard}/resources/transforms/{category}
# Place .xsl files here
----

=== Codelist dictionaries

[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p {standard}/resources/codelists
# Place codelist .xml files here
----

=== Schematron rules

Per-module rules (coupled to a specific schema version):
+
[source]
----
{standard}/{-part}/{module}/{version}/{module}.sch
----

Cross-cutting rules (apply across modules):
+
[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p {standard}/resources/schematron
# Place .sch files here
----

=== ZIP download bundles

[source,sh]
----
mkdir -p {standard}/resources/bundles
# Name as: {module}-{version}.zip
----

=== Maintenance logs

[source]
----
{standard}/resources/CHANGES.md
# Or within a subdirectory:
{standard}/resources/namespace-tools/CHANGES.md
----


== What stays where

[cols="2,4"]
|===
| Stays in version dir | Moves to resources/

| `*.xsd` — schema definitions
| `*.zip` → `resources/bundles/`

| `*.sch` — Schematron for this schema
| Codelist XML → `resources/codelists/`

| `index.adoc` — module docs
| XSL transforms → `resources/transforms/`

| `*.png` — UML diagrams
| Cross-cutting Schematron → `resources/schematron/`

| `examples/*.xml` — example instances
| Helper scripts → `resources/tools/`
|===
